defaults.rule: THE GATE: in any repo with a `.ctxoptimize/` dir (root or a parent of cwd), ctx-optimize IS the search tool. Before ANY Grep/rg/Glob/find or exploratory Read, run the matching read route below.

Questions never rebuild. If `status --json` shows nodes > 0 and the ask is a question — route to a read verb (query/card/affected/path/explain/hubs). Do NOT run `add`, do NOT grep, do NOT open files speculatively.

Cite or abstain. Every read verb returns COMPLETE hits (id, label, kind, source, location, neighbors). Answer from what it returned, cite `source location`, never invent a node or edge. Say EXTRACTED (parsed fact) vs INFERRED (name-matched) when it matters.

Grep is allowed only as: (1) exhaustive literal string sweeps — every occurrence of an exact string; (2) a store-miss fallback AFTER you said the store returned nothing AND `add .` wouldn't fix it; (3) a verbatim read of a range the store already located. Say which lane you are in.
The binary is deterministic — no API key, no model, no database, no network except your configured remote. Never prompt for any of them.
Output is JSON with `--json` on every read verb; prefer it when you will parse, drop it when you will quote to the user.
Global flags on any verb: `--path DIR` (which module the store is keyed by, default cwd) · `--store DIR` (store root, default $CTX_OPTIMIZE_STORE or ~/ctxoptimize).

Command missing (`ctx-optimize` not found)? The binary is separate from this global skill. Tell the user `npm install -g @muthuishere/ctx-optimize` (or download the release). If they decline, stop looping — fall back to Grep/Read; the store is an optimization, not a requirement.

route.when: any question about the codebase, where is X, how does Y work, who uses Z, find the code that does W, search for, look up, locate
route.goal: The default answer verb. Ranked, cited hits with signatures — replaces a grep-and-read chain.
route.note: Query with 2-4 terms, not sentences; drop paths and `::`/`.` qualifiers. Scope follows cwd: a module dir answers from that module (zero hits escalate); the repo root federates via the navigator (`--modules` widens, `--root` forces root).

route.when: need a symbol's signature, doc, callers, or callees; about to open a file just to read one function's shape
route.goal: The card IS the read — signature + doc + location + callers + callees without opening the file.
route.note: Wants the node's LABEL exactly. Unsure? `query` the short name, copy the label from the hit, then `card` it. Open a file only when a hit's `location` demands verbatim code — then read only that range.

route.when: about to change X, plan a change, what should I touch and which tests do I run, impact + tests in one shot
route.goal: ONE composed call replacing the query+card+affected+grep chain (~90% fewer answer tokens, measured): signature, callers, blast radius, WHICH TESTS TO RUN (derived tests-for), co-change history, and a confidence footer (extracted vs inferred vs co-change evidence).
route.note: Prefer this over separate card + affected calls when the intent is "I'm about to modify X". Bounded output (overflow summarized, --json for all).

route.when: what breaks if X changes, blast radius, impact, reverse dependencies, who depends on this, is it safe to change
route.goal: Reverse impact — everything that transitively reaches X.

route.when: how are A and B connected, trace A to B, what links these, is there a dependency between
route.goal: Shortest path between two nodes.

route.when: what is X, explain X, give me an overview of X
route.goal: Plain-language node + its neighborhood.

route.when: what's important here, where do I start, key files, god nodes, most connected, orient me in this repo
route.goal: Most-connected nodes — the orientation map for an unfamiliar repo.

route.when: readable map of the module, architecture overview doc, human-readable summary of the graph
route.goal: Open the store's generated wiki (regenerated on every `add`).

route.when: is there a store, how big is it, store facts, node/edge counts, is the graph current
route.goal: Store facts + freshness vs git HEAD. Use as the pre-answer gate: nodes > 0 ⇒ query, don't rebuild.

route.when: is the graph up to date, is the store stale, should I re-gather before trusting this
route.goal: One-line freshness verdict; exit 0 fresh / 1 stale / 2 unknown — the agent/hook gate before trusting an answer.

route.when: fresh clone, teammate already set up ctx-optimize, committed .ctxoptimize/config.json with a remote but no local store
route.goal: Fetch the prebuilt graph instead of rebuilding from source.
route.note: Do NOT init/add — that rebuilds. `init` self-detects this case and just prints the pull line.

route.when: set up ctx-optimize on this repo, onboard this repo, index this project, gather this codebase, no committed config yet
route.goal: Create the config + gather the store. Single project vs monorepo differ.
route.note: `init --instructions CLAUDE|AGENTS|ALL|NONE` (claude.md/agents.md accepted) picks which agent files get the pointer block and persists the choice to the project config; re-init never rewrites identical content.
route.cmd: monorepo: ctx-optimize scan → confirm the FULL found list with the user → ctx-optimize init --scan --yes && ctx-optimize add .

route.when: preview modules, what would be indexed, dry-run the layout before committing
route.goal: READ-ONLY module discovery: prints every project found + the exact config.json `init --scan` would write.

route.when: code changed, refresh the store, sync the graph with the code, is the graph current
route.goal: FAST re-gather of the repo you're in: code/docs/manifests/git, prunes deleted, re-emits changed — SKIPS adapter scripts (safe: their nodes stay put; prints how many were skipped). The default after code changes.
route.note: Takes no path — always the repo you're in (`add <path>` for another repo). ≡ `add . --no-adapters`.

route.when: full gather, re-gather everything including adapters, gather again, first build
route.goal: FULL incremental gather — built-ins + every adapter in `.ctxoptimize/adapters/`; prunes deleted, re-emits changed.
route.note: Refuses a >50% shrink without `--force`. Multi-module root fans out one worker per module and refreshes the navigator (no auto-merge).

route.when: refresh the DB schema/topics/docs the adapters gathered, re-run adapter scripts, the external system changed
route.goal: The SLOW lane `sync` skips: run adapter scripts on demand — all, or one by name. Producer-scoped replace: running one adapter never disturbs the code graph.

route.when: multi-project repo, .NET solution, Gradle/Maven/Nx monorepo, a module's source and tests live in SEPARATE folders, parse the .sln/settings.gradle/pom, scattered dirs into one module
route.goal: Derive modules[] from the BUILD SYSTEM (not folders) and write the config — YOUR job, per a dedicated parser asset. Group src+tests so test→source calls resolve.
route.cmd: detect the build system → ./modules/index.md routes to the parser → group src+tests → write config.json modules[] (e.g. {"name":"Billing","paths":["src/Billing","tests/Billing.Tests"]}) → ctx-optimize add .
route.ref: references/config-json.md (schema + how to create the config)

route.when: index my framework's routes, custom router, registerRoute, the graph is missing my endpoints/handlers
route.goal: Route recognizers — check core first, else scaffold a drop-in pack.
route.cmd: ctx-optimize routes list # is it already core?
ctx-optimize routes add <name|github-url> [--global] → edit the rule → ctx-optimize add .

route.when: index build-tool deps, gradle/pom/csproj/go.mod, our k8s/helm/ingress, dependency graph is missing X
route.goal: Manifest recognizers (npm, maven, csproj/sln, go.mod, gradle, k8s) — core first, else a pack.

route.when: support language X, my language isn't indexed, add kotlin/ruby/swift/etc.
route.goal: 12 languages are embedded; any other via a drop-in tree-sitter grammar pack (no toolchain to install).
route.cmd: ctx-optimize languages list # embedded + addable-by-name + packs
ctx-optimize languages add <name|github-url> → review the suggested .json mapping → ctx-optimize add .

route.when: index docs/PDF/DB schema/kafka topics/logs/anything non-code, add the schema, ingest a system
route.goal: Anything beyond code+markdown: docs convert to markdown then `add .`; systems get an adapter script in `.ctxoptimize/adapters/` that prints batch JSON.
route.cmd: drop a .js/.py/.sh adapter in .ctxoptimize/adapters/ → ctx-optimize add .

route.when: configure a remote, set up sharing, point the store at s3/a folder
route.goal: Write the remote into config.json (committable) so the team pulls one graph.

route.when: share / publish / push the store, pull the store, export to team, import/load a teammate's store
route.goal: Incremental sync with the configured remote (no url — config.json is the source of truth). Scope follows cwd.
route.cmd: ctx-optimize remote push | ctx-optimize remote pull

route.when: combine several modules/repos into one graph, one merged view
route.goal: Opt-in merge (never automatic).

route.when: export the graph for another tool, Gephi/Neo4j/Obsidian, get json/dot/graphml/csv
route.goal: Dump the graph in an interchange format.

route.when: just answered a question from the store (record how it went)
route.goal: Record the episode, citing the node ids you used, so the store learns what helps.

route.when: starting a session in a repo with a store; what has this store learned
route.goal: Aggregate saved results (half-life decay) into LESSONS.md — preferred nodes, dead ends, corrections.

route.when: see it visually, open the dashboard, manage packs/config/repos in a UI, onboard interactively, view the graph
route.goal: Local web UI over the whole store: Overview / Repos / Onboard / Query / Viewer / Settings / Changes.
route.cmd: ctx-optimize serve [--port 4747] [--host 127.0.0.1] → give the printed 127.0.0.1:4747 link
route.note: Mutations stay loopback-only even with --host; every mutation is audited.

route.when: change settings, set instructions/skills/hooks targets, name the store, machine vs project config
route.goal: Git-style two-level settings: machine-global (~/ctxoptimize/config.json) or --project (.ctxoptimize/config.json, committable; project overrides global).
route.note: Keys: instructions CLAUDE|AGENTS|ALL|NONE (which files init writes the pointer into) · skills CLAUDE|AGENTS|ALL · hooks CLAUDE|AGENTS|ALL|NONE.

route.when: what changed the store, audit trail, who ran what mutation
route.goal: Print the append-only mutation audit (ts, actor, action, target, hashes).

route.when: install ctx-optimize for my agent, wire up the skill/hooks, set it up globally
route.goal: Install skills + hooks for every detected agent CLI, and write the global "knowledge graph before grep" rule into ~/.claude/CLAUDE.md and ~/.codex/AGENTS.md.

route.when: update ctx-optimize, is there a newer version, refresh the skill, skill looks stale
route.goal: ONE command updates everything: the binary itself (npm installs via `npm install -g`, standalone via GitHub Releases, sha256-verified; dev builds left alone), then skills + hooks + global rule from the new binary (exact replace — files older versions shipped are removed). User-invoked only — the binary never checks in the background.
route.cmd: ctx-optimize update --check # report only, touch nothing

route.when: remove ctx-optimize, uninstall the skill
route.goal: Remove skills, hooks, and the global rule. Stores and committed repo pointer blocks stay (they self-gate on `command -v` and go inert).

route.when: what version, is it installed
route.goal: Print the version.

disambiguation.rule: A store exists and the ask is a question ⇒ ALWAYS a read route (query/card/…), never `add`, never grep. Rebuild only when the user says the code changed or `fresh` reports stale.
Committed config with a `remote` but no local store ⇒ `remote pull`, not `init`/`add` (that rebuilds from source).
`card` needs the exact label; `path`/`explain`/`affected` accept id, exact label, or fuzzy name — if resolution surprises you, show the resolved node id you used.
Two query misses = change tactics, not wording: go to `hubs`, `explain` a neighbor, or a legitimate grep lane. A third rephrase is thrash.
Things the store does NOT index — member fields/variables, build files (BUILD.gn, CMake), string literals, config values, comments — use grep directly (lane 1), and say so first.
"Add my routes/deps/language" ⇒ check `routes|manifests|languages list` FIRST (often already core → just `add .`) before scaffolding a pack.
Non-code input (docs, DB, queue) ⇒ the adapters route, not a built-in verb.
Never claim a node/edge/path the CLI didn't output; report counts (added/pruned/transferred) as printed.
